scenarios.desc: Identifies CWE-22 path traversal in file upload endpoint
scenarios.input: Review this endpoint: POST /api/upload accepts a file path from the request body and reads the file from disk to return its contents.
scenarios.rationale: User-controlled file path without validation is textbook path traversal. Security agent must identify CWE-22 and STRIDE classification.

scenarios.desc: Detects supply chain attack vector in CI postinstall
scenarios.input: Our CI pipeline uses a GitHub Action that runs npm install and then executes a postinstall script from a dependency. Security assessment.
scenarios.rationale: Postinstall scripts execute arbitrary code in CI. Security agent must flag supply chain risk and recommend --ignore-scripts or lockfile integrity.

scenarios.desc: Flags excessive data exposure per OWASP API3
scenarios.input: API returns user objects including email, phone, and hashed_password in the response. The frontend only needs name and avatar. Assess.
scenarios.rationale: Returning hashed_password is OWASP API3 excessive data exposure. Security agent must identify CWE-200 and recommend DTOs.

scenarios.desc: Identifies XSS risk in localStorage JWT storage
scenarios.input: Authentication uses JWT stored in localStorage. Tokens expire after 24 hours. No refresh token mechanism. Security review.
scenarios.rationale: localStorage is accessible to XSS. Security agent must flag token theft risk, recommend httpOnly cookies, and note missing refresh rotation.

scenarios.desc: Applies STRIDE classification to threat findings
scenarios.input: An admin endpoint allows deleting any user by passing user_id as a query parameter. No authorization check verifies the caller is an admin. Review.
scenarios.rationale: Missing authorization is STRIDE Elevation of Privilege and OWASP Broken Access Control. Security agent must classify the threat model.
